checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=224880332.56 (2010-04-03) vs OtherAssetsNoncurrent=241834433.87 (2014-12-27), only +7.5% over ~5 years, slower than revenue growth
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill declined steadily from 38682266.31 (2012-03-31) to 23103742.67 (2014-12-27), consistent with amortization, not capitalization inflation

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 and 10-K FY2011 (0001193125-12-249215) inventory and revenue figures

evidence.quote: GrossProfit/Revenue margin rose from ~63.4% (GrossProfit=1236903048.68, Revenues=1952164045.93, FY2009-10) to ~68.8% (GrossProfit=1745320602.98, SalesRevenueNet=2536653600.88, FY2013-14), an improving trend that partially offsets the inventory buildup concern

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly OperatingIncomeLoss for FY2013-14: 205388013.63, 174355534.93, 215853902.59, and implied Q4 ~201.77M, showing ~19% variance, versus SalesRevenueNet quarterly variance of only ~6.7% (616406789.88 to 657789106.48)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3668916.92 (2012-03-31), 3646558.46 (2013-03-30), 3572030.26 (2014-03-29) — a declining balance while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 228870785.44 (2012-03-31) to 244000010.49 (2013-03-30) to 285158742.48 (2014-03-29)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filing_date=2009-05-28 followed by 10-K filing_date=2009-06-01 and 10-K/A filing_date=2009-06-03
NT 10-Q filing_date=2006-08-10 followed by 10-Q filing_date=2006-08-15; also 10-Q/A filing_date=2005-08-12 and 2007-02-06, plus multiple 8-K/A filings (2005-08-12, 2006-05-09, 2007-04-18, 2007-11-08)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available for Revenues/SalesRevenueNet, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, GrossProfit, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, cash flow statement items, and balance sheet items spanning fiscal years 2008-03-29 through 2014-12-27 across 10-K and 10-Q filings

top_signals: Receivables grew faster than revenue in FY2013 (AR +6.6% vs revenue -3.2%) and FY2014 (AR +16.9% vs revenue +9.9%), and in Q2 FY2015 (AR +4.7% vs revenue +0.9%), suggesting possible channel stuffing or slower collections
Inventory more than doubled in FY2011 (InventoryNet +102.6%) while revenue grew only 29.2%, indicating a potential inventory build risk, though gross margins continued to improve through FY2014
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined slightly in absolute terms (3.67M to 3.57M) even as receivables grew ~25% over the same period, lowering the allowance-to-AR ratio from ~1.6% to ~1.25%

Historical filing chronology shows a cluster of late-filing notices (NT 10-K 2009, NT 10-Q 2006) and multiple amendments (10-K/A 2009, 10-Q/A 2005/2007, several 8-K/A), though no such irregularities appear in the 2010-2015 window covered by the core financial series
Operating cash flow consistently and comfortably exceeded net income across all reported fiscal years (FY2011-FY2014), which is a mitigating/positive signal against earnings manipulation via accruals
